title: SCOPELY / HASBRO SCAM!!!
content: The game’s fairness is seriously compromised due to its “pay-to-win” structure, making it nearly impossible to succeed without spending money. The current system heavily favors those who are willing to pay, while those who attempt to play without financial investment are at a significant disadvantage. It’s clear that, in order to be fair, there should be a realistic opportunity to win without paying, allowing all players to experience success based on skill, strategy, and time investment rather than purely on purchasing power.A balanced approach would be to allow paying players to speed up their progress or improve their chances, but not to the extent that it’s the only way to win. At present, the game lacks this balance, leaving free-to-play players discouraged and frustrated, ultimately reducing their incentive to keep playing. Fairness in gaming should mean that everyone has a viable path to success, regardless of their budget.

title: this game is not random, and i don’t like it
content: i’m good at math (board 65 within 9 days about, not really playing consistently- i’m assuming my performance speaks for something), so i notice this game is not random. there’s a pity timer, it knows where it’s going to roll, and such. i try my best to do well in this game, and it worked, at first. some might say to let up, it’s a casual game, and let others have their fair spread. there should be laws against manipulating probabilities even for virtual goods, as for gambling, just as there are laws for displaying the probabilities of virtual goods gambled with real world currency in china, and it bothers me. it bothers me enough i probably won’t be playing this game. i don’t think this kind of behavior of having outcomes or pseudo probability to maximize engagement in a game should be encouraged.also there’s a glitch where you can see the next roll. it had nothing to do with my performance but you should probably fix what information goes to the client before it becomes a problem. but don’t let me tell you how to do your job like i can do it better alright.

title: Monopoly GO the CASINO
content:
This is quite literally the epitome of monopoly, a monopoly on the users who play this “game.” Monopoly GO has proven it to me that I’ve been affected financially and emotionally over a stupid, pointless game. In retrospect, the app developers have created a scheme for which it preys on impulsive and irresponsible people who are just simply bored.

Truth is, this game should be titled Monopoly GO to the CASINO. The endless advertisements as soon as you open the game and when you run out of rolls are all opportunities for them to ruin you financially. They psychologically figured out that if you didn’t play their version of Monopoly well to your advantage, they can take your money so it could keep happening. No matter which way you play their game, all roads would lead you to spend money when you shouldn’t. For example, when you run out of dice, they run targeted ads to see if you’ll bite and run your credit card to add more dice—the cycle repeats itself. You also have to wait up to 10 hours, pretty much over half of an awakened day to play again. If you have a high roller, it literally looks like you’ve entered a casino, and the game recognizes that not everyone is smart playing this game. I’ve done this a handful of times and when nothing has went my way, I’d surely get upset, and this is when I stopped playing. You have to figure out how to play a game that is simply rigged against you. The tournament itself is rigged because you got cheaters who have modded the game to get seemingly unlimited rolls, the app doesn’t do anything about it. You also have people in this tournament who will play so last minute, even of the hour of the ending of it, who take the lead from you, take your skills to the hole because of one’s selfishness. There’s no question in my mind that the developers or someone who’s paid highly from Monopoly themselves to beat honest players. The album feature of the game is an absolute train wreck. You get even close to finishing an album, and the game rigs against you by giving you the same stickers over and over and over again, until you give in and buy something from them. 

If you’re spending a lot on this game, you’re gambling, not really playing. 

I cannot believe that even any app store, Google or Apple would approve of these manipulative practices caused by these developers because they’re making the real money too. 

This is far from the original game, and I implore to you to find different versions of the board game, you will be tricked by this one.

title: What is happening with MGO?
content:
I used to truly enjoy this game! However, since the Marvels album start the joy has been slowly sucked out of this game. The dice rewards are no longer compensating for what was spent. Usually, with banner events and tournaments you can practically break even. That is no longer the case. It’s becoming increasingly apparent they are manipulating tiles landed on based on the type of banner event in place, which is causing more dice to be spent than can be made up. For example, if a tax, utility, and chance banner event is happening, you’ll rarely land on those tiles. But! The second the event ends and changes to a pickups event, tax, utility, and chance will be the ONLY thing you land on.

Additionally, they’ve changed the order of banner events. We usually have a pickups banner event when a new event starts. Makes it easier to get tokens and progress. They follow it with a harder banner event, like corners or tax, utility, chance, which makes it harder to get tokens. This set up worked just fine! However, they’ve all of a sudden changed to starting with harder events, which burns dice, and then switch to the easier event. Since you burned dice in the previous event, it makes it harder in the easier event. 

They DRASTICALLY reduced the amount of dice won when closing a sticker set. You no longer get MORE dice when finishing the prestige album. They’ve reduced the number of stickers in the green, yellow, and pink packs to help with the number of dupes; yet, seems like the only stickers I can get are 1-3 stars.

They’ve also changed Peg-E to be nearly impossible to finish. Peg-E and the dig event use to be my favorite events. That is no longer the case. I was so excited for haunted treasures because I have ONE gold sticker left to finish the first album. Unfortunately, I won’t be finishing the event because I have no dice, even after I spent $120, to get axes because of the event order. 

Plus, they’ve changed the way we can keep money. If you’re going to FORCE us to build so we aren’t keeping cash, you also need to implement a way of not being utterly targeted in shutdowns. Many people don’t build because they have players that come through and raze their board to the ground. I save money because of that and so I can get dice during certain events. Limit the number of times a player can actively choose to target someone. Like you do with sticker trades. A player can choose 5 people—can be the same person or 5 different people—per day to target. After they’ve chosen their 5, they have to hit whoever auto populates or they can choose random player. Its not right that people that hoard money are being punished but people that abuse the targeting feature aren’t. Yes, shutdowns are a part of the game, but when I spend 700B to build a board and someone comes through and destroys every single building because I can’t live on MGO and replace shields all the time and have to spend ANOTHER 700B to build the board back, it’s discouraging, especially since heists are now higher and more frequent. Make it make sense! If you increase one thing, you need to limit another thing that causes the main source of money drain.

Stop making changes to the game because you all are seriously making the game exponentially harder and less enjoyable. If you keep making changes like y’all are, you will lose many, many players. I know of about 20 people that have stopped playing recently because your pay for play schemes are becoming too apparent. I don’t even know if I’ll continue to play after this album. If changes aren’t made, I’ll probably be dropping the game. Please, developers, go through and READ the comments on social media. 95% of the feedback and comments are completely negative.

title: Scopely / Hasbro SCAM
content:
So they surprised my last review for whatever reason. 
The respondents of the reviews say the game is random, however it’s actually a pseudorandom number generator (PRNG).

A PRNG is a mathematical algorithm that generates a sequence of numbers that APPEAR to be random, but are actually determined by a set of initial conditions, often called a seed. While the NUMBERS ARE NOT TRULY RANDOM, they can be unpredictable enough to SIMULATE the RANDOMNESS of a dice roll.

 Why would people waste dice on an Peg-e that won’t guarantee then a sticker they’re missing? And, I HOPE you all have a wild or two in quick wins like you did two albums ago. When I first started playing there were two weeks where we could win a wild in quick wins. That was not done last album. Please bring that back, especially since it’s so hard to get stickers this album. I 100% believe Scopely has seen a drop in people trading in vaults. It took me nearly 4 weeks to get enough stars to trade in a vault. That is absolute non-sense.
————
I used to truly enjoy this game! However, since the Marvels album start the joy has been slowly sucked out of this game. The dice rewards are no longer compensating for what was spent. Usually, with banner events and tournaments you can practically break even. That is no longer the case. It’s becoming increasingly apparent they are manipulating tiles landed on based on the type of banner event in place, which is causing more dice to be spent than can be made up. For example, if a tax, utility, and chance banner event is happening, you’ll rarely land on those tiles. But! The second the event ends and changes to a pickups event, tax, utility, and chance will be the ONLY thing you land on. Additionally, they’ve changed the order of banner events. We usually have a pickups banner event when a new event starts. Makes it easier to get tokens and progress. They follow it with a harder banner event, like corners or tax, utility, chance, which makes it harder to get tokens. This set up worked just fine! However, they’ve all of a sudden changed to starting with harder events, which burns dice, and then switch to the easier event. Since you burned dice in the previous event, it makes it harder in the easier event. They DRASTICALLY reduced the amount of dice won when closing a sticker set. You no longer get MORE dice when finishing the prestige album. They’ve reduced the number of stickers in the green, yellow, and pink packs to help with the number of dupes; yet, seems like the only stickers I can get are 1-3 stars. They’ve also changed Peg-E to be nearly impossible to finish. Peg-E and the dig event use to be my favorite events. That is no longer the case. I was so excited for haunted treasures because I have ONE gold sticker left to finish the first album. Unfortunately, I won’t be finishing the event because I have no dice, even after I spent $120, to get axes because of the event order. Plus, they’ve changed the way we can keep money. If you’re going to FORCE us to build so we aren’t keeping cash, you also need to implement a way of not being utterly targeted in shutdowns. Many people don’t build because they have players that come through and raze their board to the ground. I save money because of that and so I can get dice during certain events. Limit the number of times a player can actively choose to target someone. Like you do with sticker trades. A player can choose 5 people—can be the same person or 5 different people—per day to target. After they’ve chosen their 5, they have to hit whoever auto populates or they can choose random player. Its not right that people that hoard money are being punished but people that abuse the targeting feature aren’t. Yes, shutdowns are a part of the game, but when I spend 700B to build a board and someone comes through and destroys every single building because I can’t live on MGO and replace shields all the time and have to spend ANOTHER 700B to build the board back, it’s discouraging, especially since heists are now higher and more frequent. title: Fun…..a year ago
content: Literally the best free to play game ever. Fun, addicting, and F A I R. Remarkably fair. Now, as with everything, corporate greed has taken something fun and fisted it into oblivion with the desperate, whorish need for 1 more $0.99 purchase. Events are unplayable now. What used to be challenging events that free to play players had a decent chance at completing with luck and grinding are now impossible to complete past about 60% of the way due to predatory RNG and unrealistic amounts of dice needed to have a CHANCE to finish. Sticker albums are there simply to taunt you and make you hate every decision in your life that led to download this game. What used to be challenging collect a thons that saw a grind get you within 4-5 stickers, with luck and event wins enough to carry you over the top are now TOTALLY impossible to complete without either cheating or spending a minimum of $100-$500 real world dollars. Sticker packs used to contain more stickers. Packs that contain more than 1 or 2 star cards were plentiful and easy to earn. Weekly reward packs GUARANTEED 1 5-star and a new sticker. Now, no new sticker guarantee and ENDLESS duplicates of untradeable gold stickers. Don’t waste your time. Downloading this game now is like betting on the 2024 patriots to win a Super Bowl…….the golden era has past. All that remains is the burnt out husk content in milking every remaining cent out of a dying franchise.

title: Cynical “Cash Grab”
content:
I have been playing this game for a while. Over this time I have seen constant tinkering by the developers to make it progressively unlikely for players to earn either Monopoly Cash or additional dice rolls without buying them.  The changes go from obvious (like the recent change to the coin drop game (which was already clearly non-random) by removing one of the two reward targets, to harder to see (such as tinkering with algorithms to manage dice rolls based on your purchase history.

I find it incredible that they seem to think that they need to be increasingly stingy with valueless items in the game such as dice rolls and monopoly cash.  Winning these items are the only ways to move through the levels in the game.  Moving through levels is the only way a player can enjoy the game.  Tinkering with the odds and forcing players to pay indicates EXACTLY how little they care about players, long term players especially.

Well, they have successfully reached the point where it is stupid to continue playing.  Congratulations, count me out!

title: Greed has ran this game into the ground.
content:
If you don’t plan on opening your wallet, your gameplay here is gong to be very limited (as in 5 minutes or less). 

They have truly ran this game into the ground in so many ways - the latest example is this new version of Peg-E.  The maximum point value based on a 5x multiplier is only 250 points and for this experiment I only played with a 5x multiplier. 95% of the drops go to the outer two slots (either 5 points or 35) and with a day left and playing everyday I’ve only made 50% progress on the rewards. This is also the first time they’ve done all sticker pack rewards which you’d think would be an opportunity to idk - help people get stickers they don’t have. Literally every sticker pack I’ve gotten from the rewards progress at the bottom of the screen or just from the center rewards option when it occasionally actually goes towards the center and not the outer edges has been card packs I already own. So essentially this whole mini game has been a colossal waste of time and effort. 

The more you play the game the more you’ll realize both of these themes - game play is super repetitive. There’s a ton of different ways to “win” prizes but the thing they all have in common is you get garbage prizes that don’t match the multipliers at all. On team events this is compounded because you may have 4 people you’re playing with  to and these events are all driven by currencies whether it be flags or tokens around the board which essentially ties these into dice rolls which they still have yet to address why the cap is 100 at a time (which is nothing with you play with a 10x multiplier - it’s like 2 minutes of game play and 10 hours of waiting). 

The problem fundamentally with this game is you don’t create a compelling enough case to actually WANT to spend money in the game. Based on what I’ve experienced as a “free player” I don’t think a lot of the issues and challenges are going to magically improve because you throw a few bucks at the game and sadly I think there are people throwing whole paychecks at this game that haven’t figured this out. Whether it be the setup with the dice or the fact that you can literally get 250 sticker packs (and mind you these only stack when they’re the same) - MULTIPLE TIMES - swap them and get more of the same of what you have - these are repetitive themes in the game. Don’t let them fool you with these jackpots that look good because all the other prizes are garbage (ie 500 dice instead of 25, 50 or 75) - you’ll likely get the lower dice amount or land on a sticker pack and get something you already own.

title: Was fun. Now a farce!
content:

Everyone I know using this app has deleted their accounts and uninstalled. I have too. So, no, developers I will not enjoy exploring the new strategic elements. You can’t con me. I’m not that stupid. This game used to be fun until the developers decided to change the algorithm and completely drain your net worth in bank heists. They claim that it is a fair and balanced game but it isn’t at all. But I worked HARD for that money and should be able to choose what I want to do with it. Not be forced to spend it because some stupid developers think I should. AND what am I going to spend it on?! I’ve lost over half my net worth and the landmarks are $50-$60million a piece! Screw that. This game is not fun anymore. At all. Instead it’s a frustrated mess overrun with bots and third-party software. It’s a joke and I’m going to uninstall. This garbage of a game is not worth all the frustration. Thumbs down. title: Addicting until they turn the screws
content:
Most unfairly run game I’ve ever seen. Their sticker packs are no doubt variable probability. If you find success in tournaments, they continually place you in harder ones to ensure that you cannot enjoy the game without paying money. Their events get increasingly difficult as the sticker album time comes towards the end.  Any event they have to earn cool stuff also becomes unobtainable towards the end just so they can milk and last money you may have out of you when you realize that you’re only a couple points short. The odds literally change on the most simple tasks over time. I’m sure of it.  I have an mathematics and engineering degree and have recorded the probability of certain outcomes happening in their game and have determined it’s impossible if a simple random number generator was used to simulate ‘dice rolls’ or ‘wheels spinning’. They no doubt actively change their bogus algorithm to either throw you a bone when you’re pulling away from playing or tighten the screws when they see you’re dedicated and playing a lot. No reason to give the person who’s hooked on it any better luck because they’ll keep playing anyways. 

Now they’ve even made all the top rewards terrible . Swap pack is actual trash. The top of the board rewards are increasingly bad as you collect. I just collected 2700 ‘tokens’ to get 1900 dice, that’s not very balanced but okay, I can live with it. However, I can’t accept having to get 800 tokens to unlock 40 minutes of ‘mega heist’. Nor do I have any interest in playing the next 2 days and collecting another 1000 tokens for $10.4 bn.  I’ve spent too much money and time on this game. I guess I should thank this company for taking all the fun and incentives out of it. I no longer have any reason to play or pay another $1. 

Thank you for giving me my sanity back. 

DON’T DOWNLOAD GAME AND DON’T SPEND A RED PENNY. IT’S RIGGED AND A WASTE OF YOUR TIME.

title: Worse and worse and…WORSE
content:
Worse. This is the progression of the game. First they make the bank heists less unfair by increasing the likelihood you’ll will the middle tier reward. This was done by reducing from 4 diamond rings, 4 stacks of cash, and 4 coins to 4 diamond rings, 5 stacks of cash, and 3 coins. Then they reduced the likelihood of earning a blue sticker pack from the spin wheel. They did this making the reward a vault, with varying prizes. Then they introduce the awful racing partner competition…where one team of three gets a horrible prize of a measly 3,500 dice. THEN they ruin the only game I literally ever am always able to complete…the archeologist event. They did this by introducing blocks that require multiple hits to break…this game really is so horrible from when I started playing that it feels like a chore. I have 12,000 dice just sitting there and I don’t really play anymore because the game is so boring and unfair.

And all they’ll say when and if they respond to this is that they’ll pass it along to their evil developers…it’s unfair to call them evil since it’s probably all at the direction of a handful of people. But it’s hyperbole!

Update:
I came back to try the game after 4/5 months. Ya it’s worse AGAIN. I kid you not, watched the wheel slow down was about to land on mega. Then inexplicably SPEED UP? Do a whole other lap around the wheel and land on the slice after the highest reward. There’s gotta be a class action I can sign up for somewhere right?

title: Straight scam
content: As you can see from the many 1 star reviews, this is a scam. Please do not spend any sort of money on this game. We play the side challenges all the time. I spent money on dice and my challenges are significantly harder. I have photos of friends and families results and mine. title: Don’t get it unless you want to pay to play!
content: App is free, but don’t let that fool you.  Game used to be really fun and exciting to play. That was when you can actually win tournaments and events without needing to pay for dice due to always running out. They have reduced the amount of cards you get in each pack after this last update, citing that it would “benefit” players and you would get less duplicates.  That couldn’t be further from the truth. This is simply just a cash grab for them, as they’ve made this game almost impossible to complete any partner events or special events without needing to buy dice. And if you expect to complete sets good luck. Completing the album is near impossible with all the duplicates you get. Rarely will you pull a new card from a pack. It’s a shame.  I cannot play anymore. But here’s a $99 bundle for 3,400 dice and a purple pack…like what? That’s more than the price anticipated for grand theft auto 6 on consoles.  Insane.

title: Fun, until the game blatantly cheats you
content:
This is the absolute first game that I’ve ever played that I recommend everyone to avoid.  

I have played this game for multiple months - and on occasion felt that the game cheated me out of awards/rewards — but gave the game the benefit of the doubt.  Until the most recent “partners challenge” where you pay so many points to spin the wheel and you can actually feel the ticks of the spinner (on the iPhone) and as it slows on favorable results - it speeds up to land you on the worst results.

When I went to report the problem - they explained that my “lesser points” were adequately recorded — after I read that others had the same concerns.  

The game is not the same as the board game you remember - it is all about making alliances with new folks (that you can’t correspond with) only to be forced to rob from them — thus making you adversaries.  

All things considered - I’m leaving the game — as I’m guessing many others have already done (seeing as how they have had all of their properties robbed - but haven’t even tried to recover them — you will see me in that list as of tomorrow).

title: Over it
content:
Edit- I’ll just keep adding to my negative review everytime this game screws me over I guess. I reached out to customer service today because I didn’t get a full reward and they claim they don’t see it in their history. WHY WOULD I REACH OUT IF IT DIDNT HAPPEN?? Let’s say I am lying and I’m trying to get FAKE MONEY from the game…just give it to me?? Like do they lose money out of their paychecks for wrongfully giving away FAKE MONEY?????? I wasn’t lying and I spend alot of my REAL MONEY in this game which I know if my own problem but I actually enjoyed this game for a long time but it’s just totally rigged.  If you are reading this delete the game. Write your own review. They have so many fake reviews to cover these up it’s gross. 

I’ve been playing this game for a few months now and I’ve been getting more and more disappointed. The events and tournaments are harder to get top prizes (due to the game being rigged- ex: never landing on tiles you need more and more, spinning low numbers in partners events despite using a large multiplier, bots beating you in tournaments, only getting gold cards you can’t trade, not hitting any points in peg-e…etc) people on the trading groups are extremely greedy because no one can get good cards anymore, customer service is fake (only sends automated responses and even deletes conversation), also more than half of the reviews on here are blatantly fake or paid for. It’s so disheartening because this game was actually fun and addicting and I would get all my friends to play. But now I’m honestly just over it and I know many people who share the same sentiments. I never write reviews but I’m doing this as a plea to the creators to stop making this game so difficult and let us have fun again. I haven’t deleted the app because I really did enjoy this game and have spent a significant amount $$ (which I regret since it didn’t get me anywhere). Taking a break for now and hopefully there will be changes soon if not I’ll probably delete and I know many others are feeling the same way.

title: They Just Want Your Money
content: I wanted to love this game, I really did. But the developers make it impossible to win events and complete albums without spending money. Their packages are not cheap, so be prepared to drop some money if you want the accomplishment of finishing an event or album. To make things worse, you could actually WIN a prize on an event only to not be awarded the points after all. This happened to me. I had a mega spin for 25k points that would open a vault for my partner and I, but I wasn’t given the points. My tokens were taken from me for the spin but I didn’t get any points. Customer service pretends to care but all they do is send out canned responses. I know this because other people have shared screenshots of their customer service responses. They do not care if you’ve lost a prize. They make sad attempts to pacify you by giving you more tokens so you can continue playing the event, but absolutely will not award you the prize that you rightfully earned. Why? I can only guess that it’s to urge you to buy more so you can play more to get the prize. They claim it goes against their fair play rules to award prizes if there’s a glitch, but the glitch in my case was NOT getting the prize! My advice is to invest your time and energy on another game to at isn’t all about you spending money—a game that’s enjoyable where you can win without having to empty your pockets!

Scopely will “ban” your account for “violating their terms and conditions” and prompt you to contact support if this is a mistake… then they ignore your message. All your game play, dice, and tokens will be gone. Even if you spent money on the game! Scopely is not a company worth playing for. They do anything to try to get you to spend hundreds of dollars on a couple hundred dice and a sticker pack… Don’t give them the business. Don’t waste your time and -definitely- don’t waste your money. I said what I said.

Edit** I forgot to add! When you ask them to delete your account, before they will do so they ask for receipts for purchases you made in the game… and if you don’t delete your account your game will continue to play for you after you are banned. Your friends can still attack your landmarks and heist your bank and you will still attack them- but as a bot…. The way this game is run shouldn’t be legal.

 I promise you will either lose a ton of money or delete the game because you can’t get anywhere. Just don’t do it. So many better games out there with less crappy developers.

title: Was great then Scopely got greedy
content: This game used to be so much fun, you could actually compete in and finish challenges, events, and albums.  Now, you only get 100 rolls every 10 hours…. you heard me), you can’t get enough rolls to compete or complete in any challenges or events, new stickers are hard to come by (I haven’t had a new sticker in a pack for going on 6 weeks), and now the algorithms have changed so that you will land on a “reward” spot that helps you with challenges/events in only 1 of 10-12 rolls.  So, doing math……if you get 100 per 10 hours, and you only roll 1 at a time, you will land on 10 reward spots (and believe me you absolutely CANNOT progress or win anything that way when it takes 1200+ ‘points’ to earn the reward).   Guaranteed if you let people win a lot more often they’d buy your ridiculously small packs so they can finish the events or challenges because they were close enough the packs could help.  As it is now I would have to buy at least 2 if not 4 of the most expensive packs at $99 just to half finish anything.  Scopely you’re going the way of Mobile Strike……you got insanely popular, then you got greedy, now you’re going to run off all your most loyal players and fade into obscurity.

title: THE WORST GAME EVER!! ZERO STARS
content:
Monopoly Go is one of the most frustrating and unrewarding mobile games I’ve ever played. At first it gets you addicted, but don’t be fooled — the deeper you go, the more you realize it’s a thinly disguised cash grab.

Completing card packs and mini-games is impossible unless you’re willing to spend a fortune. Even if you play every single day, multiple times a day, you’re left spinning for minimal progress. The rewards you earn rarely make a dent, and the odds of getting the cards you actually need are ridiculously low. It feels like the game is designed to dangle just enough progress to make you feel like you might succeed, but ultimately pushes you toward microtransactions.

The daily grind offers no meaningful payoff. Whether it’s rolling dice or participating in limited-time events, it’s incredibly repetitive and leaves you wondering why you’re wasting your time. The gameplay isn’t satisfying enough to justify the hours it demands — and without spending money, you’ll fall far behind those who can afford to buy their way to success.

If you value your time or wallet, avoid this app. It’s a great example of a game that’s more interested in emptying your bank account than delivering a rewarding experience. Skip it.

title: They keep making the game worse
content:
Don’t start playing this game and save yourself from the inevitable frustration that comes with playing.

I’m been playing this game for over a year and as you play the game they keep making changes that make the game worse. There used to be a guaranteed sticker pack-they got rid of it. You use to be able to get a wild sticker in the highest vault-they removed it. The dig game used to be 20 levels-now it’s 25. There used to be 10 minute high roller, gold chance card, and roll match rewards in the partner event-they removed all three. There used to be high roller twice a day every day-not anymore. They changed the bank heights and people got months of money they saved stolen. There used to be 3 pink packs you could get in every top event-now most of the season you can only get two. You used to be able to use strategy to get in lower bracket tournaments-I haven’t been able to do that at all this season. They reduced the amount of stickers in the green pack making it harder to open a vault. There’s even more but as someone who has stuck around for a long time hoping for improvements know that the game is designed to pressure you to spend money. I understand an app needs to make money however the prices are absolutely ridiculous. If you spend money on a sticker pack, you have a chance of getting a sticker you need with no guarantee you will get it. If you have plenty of spare money to waste then this is the game for you, otherwise don’t bother.

They introduced wild cards which helped balance out that they don’t do a Golden Blitz for every gold card and instead of making them attainable for every player, they stuck them at the end of events that are nearly impossible to complete for your average player. Unless you have money to burn the game just isn’t worth it anymore. Highly disappointed that such a fun game has devolved into something this bad. Being two cards away from a token and unable to get them no matter what you do is immensely frustrating. Scopely has focused more on creating unaffordable “deals” and impossible to complete events to try and wring every dime out of its player base that it can.

title: They keep making the game worse
content:
Don’t start playing this game and save yourself from the inevitable frustration that comes with playing. I’m been playing this game for over a year and as you play the game they keep making changes that make the game worse. There used to be a guaranteed sticker pack-they got rid of it. You use to be able to get a wild sticker in the highest vault-they removed it. The dig game used to be 20 levels-now it’s 25. There used to be 10 minute high roller, gold chance card, and roll match rewards in the partner event-they removed all three. There used to be high roller twice a day every day-not anymore. They changed the bank heights and people got months of money they saved stolen. There used to be 3 pink packs you could get in every top event-now most of the season you can only get two. You used to be able to use strategy to get in lower bracket tournaments-I haven’t been able to do that at all this season. They reduced the amount of stickers in the green pack making it harder to open a vault. There’s even more but as someone who has stuck around for a long time hoping for improvements know that the game is designed to pressure you to spend money. I understand an app needs to make money however the prices are absolutely ridiculous. If you spend money on a sticker pack, you have a chance of getting a sticker you need with no guarantee you will get it. If you have plenty of spare money to waste then this is the game for you, otherwise don’t bother.

In response to the response: The odds were always difficult and the game made it worse. If anyone reads this review don’t listen to Scopely they don’t care about player satisfaction or retention and only care about the money in your wallet.

title: Monopoly Go Review: Glitches, Scams, and Horrible Support
content:
Monopoly Go had potential, but my experience with this game has been nothing short of a nightmare. The glitches are rampant, and the so-called “customer support” is useless. For days, I’ve been asking for assistance after a suspicious URL appeared at the bottom of my game screen, which makes me think someone within the company might be attempting to hack accounts. Instead of answers, I’ve been passed from one generic response to another, with no resolution.

To make matters worse, I paid for dice and never received them. When I raised the issue, they outright lied, claiming I wasn’t charged—even though I have proof. This isn’t just frustrating; it’s theft. Add to that the missing dice rolls during the last event—another issue I reported that remains unsolved—and it’s clear this game is a mess.

The worst part? The lack of accountability. Players who invest their time and money in this game deserve better, but the developers don’t seem to care. Monopoly Go is plagued with technical issues and outright dishonesty, and I would strongly caution anyone against downloading or spending money on it. Save yourself the frustration—this game is not worth your time or money.

title: From Fun to Frustration
content:
What once felt like a fresh and enjoyable pastime has devolved into a frustrating money pit, riddled with greed and poor customer service.

The game initially provided a sense of accomplishment through strategy and perseverance. Now, progress feels impossible unless you’re willing to open your wallet. Completing albums, a key element of the game, has become a costly endeavor. Without spending real money, players are left scrambling, only to fall short due to rigged odds and unfair mechanics.

If you encounter an issue or voice concerns, don’t waste your time reaching out to Monopoly GO’s customer support. Every interaction feels robotic, with agents responding using copy-pasted, generic replies. There’s no resolution, no real acknowledgment of problems, and certainly no effort to ensure player satisfaction. 

The game encourages players to participate in tournaments, events, and features like the Golden Blitz to earn rewards. However, these competitions feel blatantly unfair. It’s nearly impossible to succeed when you’re pitted against bots and APM users. Support’s vague advice to “win more events” is laughable when the odds are so obviously stacked against you.

Scopely, seems to have prioritized profit over player experience. The shift is glaringly obvious, and it’s deeply disappointing for long-time players. What could have been a fun and fair game is now a glaring example of corporate greed.

Unless you’re ready to spend significant amounts of money just to keep up, this game is not worth your time. It fails to honor fairness, lacks meaningful support, and prioritizes profit over enjoyment. For those who value a balanced and enjoyable gaming experience, look elsewhere. Here’s hoping the reviews continue to decline until Scopely takes notice and makes meaningful changes.

title: Purchase with caution.

You should really hire people in other time zones because why am I getting responses at 11:30 at night? 

PURCHASE WITH CAUTION. I’ve been waiting to hear back about an overcharge. I only planned to pay maybe $10 or so and they charged me nearly $20. Their customer service isn’t readily available so I’m not able to hear back within the next few hours. I was close to winning an event so I figured $10 wouldn’t hurt. Not to mention they charged the wrong card. 

The events are insufferable and if you want to win you have to either pay a lot of money or hoard dice overtime. It’s not a viable option to just roll your regular 100 when you’re really high leveled. There needs to be some sort of scaling change because it’s not fun to play anymore. In the beginning sure, but just feels like unless you’re rolling x20 minimum (if that) you will gain nothing.

title: Monopoly GO! - A Fun Game Ruined by Shady Practices
content:
Monopoly GO! used to be my go-to game, but sadly, it has become a frustrating experience. Recently, players—whether they followed the rules or not—have been unfairly banned from their accounts with no option to appeal. It’s like they’re wiping out their current player base just to make room for new players drawn in by their flashy, celebrity-packed commercials.

On top of that, the game has made it nearly impossible to enjoy or complete mini-games, tournaments, or top events without spending real money on dice. It feels like they’ve intentionally ramped up the difficulty to push players toward purchases, all while ignoring the legitimate concerns of their long-time fans.

The lack of transparency and refusal to allow appeals for bans is disappointing, to say the least. What was once a fun, community-driven game now feels like a cash grab. Proceed with caution—this game may seem fun at first, but the shady practices are a major letdown.

⭐️ 0/5 Stars

title: Subject: Urgent Appeal Regarding Unjust Bans in Monopoly Go
content:
Dear Monopoly Go Customer Service Team,
I am writing to express my profound disappointment and frustration regarding the recent bans that have been imposed on players, myself included, within Monopoly Go. This situation has left a bitter taste in what should be a fun and engaging experience.
Imagine investing your time and energy into a game, only to find your progress abruptly halted by an unseen hand, casting you into a shadow of uncertainty and confusion. The lack of clarity surrounding the banning process feels like a storm cloud looming over our community, creating an atmosphere of fear and suspicion. Many players, myself included, feel as though we are victims of an arbitrary system that punishes rather than protects.
The game's mechanics themselves often seem to encourage players to resort to questionable tactics just to keep up, which only adds to the frustration. It feels as though we are navigating a maze with shifting walls, where each turn leads to potential pitfalls rather than the joyful competition we all crave.
As a passionate player, I urge you to reflect on the impact of these bans and the atmosphere they create. Restoring trust and integrity within the Monopoly Go community is essential for ensuring that all players can enjoy the game without the looming threat of unjust penalties.
I hope for a prompt and thoughtful response that addresses these pressing concerns.
Thank you for your attention to this matter.

content: Don’t waste your time or money. Game is not set up for dynamic gameplay. title: Not recommended at this time….
content:
First, let’s talk about the mini-game with the slot machine robot. It’s rigged. If your coin touches the prize ball at least three times, the coin is deliberately designed to land in the lowest-value slot. For example: you drop a coin worth 1x, and if it hits a prize ball three or more times, it will fall into the 1-point slot. This makes it frustratingly difficult to win any meaningful prizes or walk away with anything valuable since you have no control over where the coin lands. This ruins the fun and discourages me from spending money in-app, knowing the game is designed this way.

Secondly, I absolutely hate the new addition of dice as a prize. Why dice? No one can see which dice you use, so it feels pointless. The shields and tokens make sense because other players can see them—it lets people know who’s most active and who’s better to pair up with or play alongside. But adding dice to the prize pool reduces the chances of winning items we actually care about. It’s already hard enough to win stickers and other coveted prizes, and now with the additional dice prizes, it’s even harder.

Next, I’d like to express my concern about the new net worth update. The prizes for leveling up are trash! It feels like a slap in the face as a player to keep progressing, only to see the rewards getting worse. I reached a point in the game where my next two levels should have rewarded me with 2,500 rolls total, which I was looking forward to because I knew a mini-challenge (I’ve learned the game’s rotation) was coming up. Instead, I got no rolls at all. Instead, I received a useless rent upgrade on one level and cash on the next. To me, the 2,500 rolls were far more valuable—and I’m assuming the developers felt the same way, or they wouldn’t have downgraded the prizes.

Another grievance: the game should recognize when a player no longer needs low-value stickers, like those rated at 1-star. At some point, the game should stop offering green sticker prizes because they become a waste. Don’t agree? Maybe you’d argue they can be saved for future prizes. But with dice now added to the prize mix, it’s even harder to collect stickers. The least the developers could do is make it easier to collect stickers that matter. Many players accumulate over a thousand useless stickers during the event period, yet we only need 150–180 specific stickers to win the big prize. I know this firsthand because I won the 450-overage sticker box twice and the 250-overage box once during this round—and still couldn’t complete the challenge. It may be more helpful if the 450 and 700 prize boxes contained a swap prize or a pick a sticker especially since it will be harder to get these anyways with the prize addition you’ve added to the mix. 

Just an idea: players who are consistently inactive should not be invited to team-based mini-games. These games should be reserved for active players. It’s unfair when a teammate ghosts their team. The game should have a feature to identify inactive players and allow the team to replace them. Alternatively, inactive players shouldn’t be allowed to collect prizes earned solely through their teammates’ efforts. People who put 0 effort in should not be winning anything, or at least the prize should become available to win until the inactive player plays. For example, people who play Monopoly in the background as their teammates races other teams and only clicks in to collect prizes won but never plays, are people who should be up for replacement.  Maybe even make it more fun and allow players to steal teammates.

title: Unethical, Unfair, and a Privacy Concern
content:
If I could give zero stars, I would. My experience with Monopoly Go has been frustrating and deeply concerning. I’ve been playing for almost a year but the game has find downhill significantly starting in August. In hopes of saving others the trouble I will point out 3 huge concerns with this game: 
1.Unacceptable Customer Service: I had numerous interactions with customer service regarding crashes, lost items, and system failures. Customer service always leaves generic responses and never helps or offer resolutions- but always is “aware” of the situation. What’s worse- I was banned without notice, along with my entire family, despite being a paying customer. Attempts to get answers were met with copy-and-paste responses, offering no clarity or resolution. Even when requesting information or clarity they copy and paste the exact same message- not a single letter different. It’s clear the customer service team prioritizes brushing off concerns over addressing them. They are banning people in large masses without explaining how or why and several are left baffled. 
2.Sketchy Use of Data: The email address I provided exclusively for this game was sold to third parties—proven by the influx of related spam from Monopoly Go affiliates. When I brought this to customer services attention- they told me to review their policies and that I agreed to this! I asked to be removed immediately and they had my jump through a bunch of hoops rather then removing the email. I was never successful in disconnecting my email from the account and now my account is banned but still being used by the developers as a bot!!! Meanwhile, the game prohibits “third-party trading apps” (like Sticker Go and Sticker Hub), labeling them as “cheating.” When I inquired about these apps customer service informed me they are considered cheating even though the game encourages trading with no embedded ways to find trades. Instead, they push players to use their own unmoderated and scam-filled platforms, like Discord and their Facebook page. All of which are breathing nightmares to maneuver through. 
3.Pay-to-Progress Scheme: Progress in this game is blatantly designed to require spending money. Even after spending, my family was banned with no justification. The randomness in the game is an illusion—it’s clearly programmed to push players toward in-app purchases. Everyone will conveniently only need one more sticker before end of the season when a large push for a wild sticker will pop up for ridiculous amounts of money. 

Final Thoughts: Monopoly Go is a money grab wrapped in an unethical system. It misuses data, lacks transparency, and manipulates players into 
spending. I wouldn’t be surprised if Scopely faces a class-action lawsuit in the future. Save your time, money, and data/privacy—don’t play this game.
